#140 🔤 Word Break II HARD Backtracking + Memo
exp: 2 sentences

📝 Problem Statement

Given a string s and a dictionary wordDict, add spaces in s to construct a sentence where every word exists in the dictionary. Return all possible sentences (in any order). Same word can be reused.

🧠 Solving Approach

Use DFS + Memoization. From every starting index, try every possible word that matches the prefix. If it matches, recurse on the remaining suffix and combine the results. Cache the list of all valid sentences starting from each index to avoid recomputation.

Base Case When start == n return [""] (empty sentence seed)
Pruning Only try prefixes present in wordDict. Memo avoids re-computation.
Key Join with space only when child sentence is non-empty.
Time: O(N² × 2^N) worst Space: O(N × 2^N)

🧪 Test Cases (click to load)

T1 2 sentences
s="catsanddog" ["cat","cats","and","sand","dog"]
T2 3 sentences
s="pineapplepenapple" ["apple","pen","applepen","pine","pineapple"]
T3 0 sentences
s="catsandog" ["cats","dog","sand","and","cat"]
T4 many
s="aaaaaaa" ["aaaa","aa","a"]
—
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization — Step-by-Step Dry Run
📝 Input String + Current Position
📚 Recursion Stack (dfs calls)
🧩 Current Building Path (words chosen)
📝 Level Partials (res[] at current start)
🗂️ Memo Cache (start → sentences)
✅ Output — Valid Sentences (Result)
Current start Trying word Matched / Confirmed Stack / Partial 📍 active • ✅ done • 🟡 visiting • ❌ pruned
0/0 INIT
←→ 100%
